summary: DHH shares an O'Reilly ONLamp Rails tutorial and discusses the 10x productivity claim for Rails over Java frameworks. He acknowledges being hesitant about exact productivity multipliers but notes the 10x figure has been echoed by Java developers switching to Rails. He clarifies that project completion involves more than just programming, so the multiplier doesn't apply to entire projects. He encourages other Rails developers to publish their knowledge through outlets like O'Reilly.
key_points: The 10x productivity claim for Rails over Java has been corroborated by Java developers who switched
DHH is careful to distinguish programming speed from overall project completion speed
Publishing Rails knowledge through established outlets like O'Reilly helps spread adoption
money_quotes: What would you think if I told you that you could develop a web application at least ten times faster with Rails than you could with a typical Java framework?
I'm always hesitant to provide any exact measures of productivity increases, but the 10x number has been echoed by more than a few of the Java programmers I've known coming over.
Most projects involve lots more than just programming, so this would hardly mean a project completed 10x.
Let this serve as an encouragement for other Railers to spread the good work by getting their knowledge published by outfits like O'Reilly.
key_insight: Rails delivers dramatic productivity gains over Java frameworks in the programming phase, even if the multiplier doesn't apply to entire project timelines.
